forestry01.Indicator: 1.1.1 Proportion of the population living below the international poverty line by sex, age, employment status and geographic location (urban/rural)
forestry01.SDG_Target: 1.1 By 2030, eradicate extreme poverty for all people everywhere, currently measured as people living on less than $1.25 a day
forestry01.Description: Refer to E-handbook on SDG indic https://unstats.un.org/wiki/display/SDGeHandbook/Goal+1

forestry02.Indicator: 1.2.1 Proportion of population living below the national poverty line, by sex and age
forestry02.SDG_Target: 1.2 By 2030, reduce at least by half the proportion of men, women and children of all ages living in poverty in all its dimensions according to national definitions
forestry02.Description: Refer to E-handbook on SDG indic https://unstats.un.org/wiki/display/SDGeHandbook/Goal+1

forestry03.Indicator: Change in SOC stock due to activities implemented in project activity
forestry03.SDG_Target: 2.4 By 2030, ensure sustainable food production systems and implement resilient agricultural practices
forestry03.Description: Refers to the change in SOC level due to sustainable soil management practices implemented in the reporting period

forestry04.Indicator: Change in farmer income as a result of project, by gender and indigenous status
forestry04.SDG_Target: 2.3 By 2030, double the agricultural productivity and incomes of small-scale food producers
forestry04.Description: Refers to change in farmer income due to adoption of measures implemented as part of project activity
forestry04.Guidance: May be compiled using farmer surveys where detailed income surveys are unavailable
forestry04.Data Unit: Percentage increase in average annual salary

forestry05.Indicator: Area under reduced/avoided open burning of biomass, crop residue
forestry05.SDG_Target: 3.9 Reduce deaths and illnesses from hazardous chemicals and pollution
forestry05.Description: Total land area where alternative measures are implemented to avoid open biomass burning
forestry05.Guidance: Presence of baseline open burning must be established through inspection

forestry06.Indicator: Number of farmers reduced/avoided open burning
forestry06.SDG_Target: 3.9 Reduce deaths and illnesses from hazardous chemicals and pollution
forestry06.Description: Number of farmers adopting alternatives to open burning
forestry06.Guidance: Baseline open burning must be verified

forestry07.Indicator: Number of employees provided skill development training
forestry07.SDG_Target: 4.4 Increase number of youth and adults with relevant skills
forestry07.Guidance: Training categorized as skills-based, technical, governance, etc.

forestry08.Indicator: Number of training hours provided for employees, disaggregated by gender
forestry08.SDG_Target: 4.4 Increase number of youth and adults with relevant skills
forestry08.Guidance: Includes internal and external training opportunities

forestry09.Indicator: Proportion of women in managerial positions
forestry09.SDG_Target: 5.5 Ensure women’s full and effective participation
forestry09.Description: Percentage of women in managerial positions

forestry10.Indicator: Average time saving associated with cooking time and fuel collection
forestry10.Description: Time savings due to adoption of project technology
forestry10.Guidance: Refer to Gold Standard gender guidelines

forestry11.Description: Ratio of average wage paid to female employees compared to male employees

forestry12.Indicator: Number of women serving in leadership roles
forestry12.Description: Number of female managers and leaders
forestry12.Guidance: Count unique female individuals in leadership

forestry13.Description: Measurement of river water turbidity levels within the project area
forestry13.Guidance: Monitoring should compare baseline and reporting period measurements

forestry14.Description: Change in soil or landscape water retention capacity due to project activities
forestry14.Guidance: Assess via hydrological or soil moisture monitoring

forestry15.SDG_Target: 7.1 Ensure universal access to affordable, reliable and modern energy
forestry15.Description: Number of unique households provided access to clean fuels and technologies
forestry15.Guidance: May estimate using number of operational units distributed

forestry16.SDG_Target: 7.1 Ensure universal access to affordable, reliable and modern energy
forestry16.Description: Number of unique individuals provided access to clean fuels and technologies
forestry16.Guidance: Account only users actively using clean fuels

forestry17.Description: Total jobs generated as a result of project implementation
forestry17.Guidance: Disclose employees by contract type and gender

forestry18.Indicator: Total number of employees earning above local minimum wage
forestry18.Description: Number of employees earning above minimum wage
forestry18.Guidance: Provide reference to official minimum wage source

forestry19.Indicator: Total number of employees paid living wage
forestry19.Description: Number of employees receiving a living wage
forestry19.Guidance: Refer to Anker methodology or equivalent
forestry19.Data Unit: USD or local currency per month

forestry20.Description: Total work hours created by project activities
forestry20.Guidance: Report total hours equivalent labor created, disaggregated by gender

forestry21.Indicator: Economic value generated locally via employment
forestry21.SDG_Target: 8.9 Promote sustainable tourism and local economic development
forestry21.Description: Total monetary value paid towards local employment

forestry22.Indicator: Income generation from other activities (e.g., ecotourism)
forestry22.Description: Revenue generated from additional livelihood activities
forestry22.Guidance: Document tourism and other enterprise income streams

forestry23.Indicator: Average hourly earnings of employees by sex, age, occupation and disability
forestry23.Description: Mean hourly earnings disaggregated by sex and demographic characteristics
forestry23.Guidance: Calculate gender pay gap where applicable

forestry24.Indicator: Average reduction (%) in synthetic fertiliser used per ha
forestry24.Description: Percentage reduction in synthetic fertilizer usage

forestry25.Indicator: Average reduction (%) in pesticide used per ha

forestry26.Indicator: Amount of GHG emissions avoided or sequestered
forestry26.SDG_Target: 13.2 Integrate climate change measures into policies
forestry26.Description: Total greenhouse gas emissions avoided or sequestered

forestry27.Description: Total amount of non-Renewable biomass fuel saved
forestry27.Guidance: Adjust for fraction of non-Renewable biomass

forestry28.Description: Total forest area under sustainable management
forestry28.Guidance: Report area under certified or verified management

forestry29.Indicator: Total above and below ground biomass stock in forest

forestry30.Description: Area of land on which trees were planted

forestry31.Description: Total land area marked as protected
forestry31.Guidance: Report percentage of project area protected

forestry32.Indicator: Total Area of Trees Planted: Native Species
forestry32.Guidance: Report percentage of native species coverage

forestry33.Description: Number of threatened species protected in project area

forestry34.Description: Changes in species diversity within the project area

forestry35.Description: Distribution trends of planted and native species

forestry36.Indicator: Total Area under sustainable forest management
forestry36.Description: Total land area under sustainable management practices
